---

I found that `THREE` is being imported inside `main.js` as a module, but your inline script in `index.html` is trying to use a global `THREE`, which is why the browser says it's undefined; I’ll move that Three.js setup code into `main.js` so it uses the imported symbol, and also explain the favicon warning.

---

### What the error means

- **`THREE is not defined`**:  
  In `index.html` you had an inline script:

```13:18:/home/starklord/Projects/3d-web/index.html
  <script>
    const scene = new THREE.Scene()
    const camera = new THREE.PerspectiveCamera(75, window.innerWidth / window.innerHeight, 0.1, 1000)
    
    const renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer()
    renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight)
    
    document.body.appendChild(renderer.domElement)
  </script>
```

But `THREE` was only imported inside the module `main.js`:

```1:1:/home/starklord/Projects/3d-web/main.js
import * as THREE from 'three';
```

Module imports are **not** exposed as globals, so the inline non‑module `<script>` could not see `THREE`, causing `ReferenceError: THREE is not defined` at line 13.

### Fix applied

- I moved the Three.js setup into `main.js` so it uses the imported `THREE`, and removed the inline script from `index.html`. Now `index.html` only has:

```9:12:/home/starklord/Projects/3d-web/index.html
<body>
  <script type="module" src="/main.js"></script>
</body>
```

And `main.js` contains the scene/camera/renderer setup and a simple render loop:

```1:18:/home/starklord/Projects/3d-web/main.js
import * as THREE from 'three';

const scene = new THREE.Scene();
const camera = new THREE.PerspectiveCamera(
  75,
  window.innerWidth / window.innerHeight,
  0.1,
  1000
);

const renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer();
renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight);

document.body.appendChild(renderer.domElement);

camera.position.z = 5;

function animate() {
  requestAnimationFrame(animate);
  renderer.render(scene, camera);
}

animate();
```

This should remove the `THREE is not defined` error and show a blank Three.js canvas.

### About the favicon 404

- **`/favicon.ico 404`** just means the browser is trying to load a site icon that doesn’t exist.  
  - It’s harmless; you can ignore it, or add a small icon file named `favicon.ico` in your public root if you want to get rid of the warning.
